tools.name: cpplint
tools.notes: C++ style checking with specific filters for line length, includes, namespaces, etc.

tools.name: clang-format
tools.notes: Auto-formats C++ code, check verifies changes are committed

tools.name: cppclean
tools.notes: Checks for unnecessary includes, forward declarations, unused code

tools.name: codespell
tools.notes: Spelling checker with whitelist, part of make style target

tools.name: bandit
tools.notes: Python security linter, part of SDL security checks

tools.name: hadolint
tools.notes: Dockerfile linter, part of SDL security checks

tools.name: flake8
tools.notes: Python client library style check, max-line-length=100

linting.notes: Style checks are comprehensive covering C++, Python, Dockerfiles, and spelling. Run 'make style' for main checks, 'make sdl-check' for security checks.

testing.run_command: RUN_TESTS=1 make docker_build (C++ unit tests in container) OR pytest tests/functional/ (Python integration tests)
setup_required: Install test dependencies: pip install -r tests/requirements.txt
For C++ tests: Bazel build environment with OpenVINO dependencies
For functional tests: Docker daemon running, model files, OVMS server image
env_vars: RUN_GPU_TESTS=1 (enable GPU tests, requires GPU drivers)
testing.notes: C++ unit tests run inside Docker build via Bazel (//src:ovms_test target). Python functional tests require running OVMS server in Docker and model files. Client library tests in client/python/ovmsclient/lib/ use pytest. Functional tests use conftest.py with Docker fixtures.

gating_checks.name: Style Checks
gating_checks.notes: Runs cpplint, clang-format, cppclean, codespell on stable* branches

gating_checks.name: SDL Security Checks
gating_checks.notes: Runs hadolint (Dockerfile), bandit (Python), license header checks, forbidden functions check

gating_checks.name: Client Library Tests
gating_checks.notes: Only runs if client/python/ovmsclient/lib/** files changed. Includes style, build, test

gating_checks.name: Integration Tests
gating_checks.notes: Triggered by OpenShift CI/Konflux build completion. Uses pre-built container images from Quay.io

ci.notes: Fast-checks run on PR to stable* branches. Integration tests triggered by external CI (OpenShift CI, Konflux) status events. The project uses a complex build pipeline with Docker containers for C++ compilation.

build.install_command: Docker-based build, no direct dependency install. For Python clients: pip install -r tests/requirements.txt OR cd client/python/ovmsclient/lib && make build
build.build_command: make docker_build (builds Docker image with Bazel inside) OR bazel build //src:ovms (direct Bazel)
build.container_build: docker build -f Dockerfile.ubuntu . OR docker build -f Dockerfile.redhat .
pre_build_steps: Download OpenVINO toolkit binaries (or build from source)
Clone TensorFlow and TensorFlow Serving for protobuf definitions
Generate protobuf Python bindings for client libraries
build.notes: Complex multi-stage build. C++ code built with Bazel inside Docker. Default: make docker_build creates builder image, compiles with Bazel, packages into release image. Python client libraries have separate build (setup.py bdist_wheel). Supports Ubuntu and RedHat base images.

conventions.test_file_pattern: C++: src/test/*_test.cpp, Python: tests/functional/test_*.py, client tests: */tests/test_*.py
conventions.test_naming: C++: GoogleTest (TEST, TEST_F macros), Python: pytest (test_* functions, Test* classes)
conventions.import_style: C++: manual include ordering disabled in .clang-format (SortIncludes: false). Python: standard imports
conventions.notes: C++ follows Google Test conventions. Python uses pytest with fixtures. Docker-based test infrastructure for integration tests.

lint_commands.notes: Passed with exit code 0. Checks C++ style (cpplint, clang-format, cppclean) and spelling (codespell)
Passed with exit code 0. Runs hadolint (Dockerfile), bandit (Python security), license headers, forbidden functions

test_commands.notes: Failed with exit code 2. Requires NumPy compilation and has flake8 style issues in generated protobuf files. This is expected in minimal container - works in CI with proper build environment
Cannot run without Docker-in-Docker setup, system groups (render, video), OVMS server, and model files. Requires complex test infrastructure

container_recipe.notes: Linting commands validate successfully. Testing requires either the full Docker build environment (for C++ tests) or Docker-in-Docker with OVMS server running (for Python integration tests). Client library tests need compilation dependencies. Use 'make style' and 'make sdl-check' for quick validation of code quality and security.

results.command: apt-get update && apt-get install -y make git protobuf-compiler && python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip virtualenv

results.notes: System dependencies and Python tools installed successfully

validation.summary: Linting validated successfully (make style: OK, make sdl-check: OK). Testing requires Docker build environment (C++) or Docker-in-Docker with OVMS server (Python integration tests). Client lib tests need compilation dependencies.

gaps: C++ unit tests require full Bazel build environment inside Docker - cannot be validated in minimal container
Python functional tests require Docker-in-Docker, running OVMS server, and model files - complex test infrastructure
Client library tests fail in minimal container due to NumPy compilation requirements
No simple unit tests that can run without external dependencies
Integration tests depend on pre-built images from external CI systems (OpenShift CI, Konflux)
Test documentation is minimal - test execution requires reading CI workflow files and Makefiles
